ORDER RE: STIPULATION AND ORDER FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF PREMISE DATA CORPORATION'S AMENDED NOTICE AND MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. THE DEPOSITION QUESTIONS AT ISSUE ARE RELEVANT AND A PLAUSIBLE LINE OF INQUIRY. THE DEFENDANT'S ANSWERS - WHETHER DELIBERATELY OR NOT - WERE NON-RESPONSIVE TO THE KEY COMPONENT OF THE QUESTION(S) NAMELY, THE TIMING OF THE ONSET OF THE ELEMENTS OF HIS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. HE CONTINUALLY ANSWERED THE QUESTION WITH THE SUBSTANCE - OR, THE "WHAT" - OF STATEMENTS MADE TO HIS THERAPIST, BUT NEVER THE "WHEN" INSOFAR AS THE TIMING OF THE ONSET OF THOSE SYMPTOMS. THEREFORE, COUNSEL'S OBJECTIONS AND INSTRUCTIONS WERE IMPROPER AND THE PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSE. JUDGE PRO TEM: NOAH LEBOWITZ, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: RAQUEL SHARP, CSR# 10619, OF BARKLEY COURT REPORTERS, (TEL: 925-381-5000; EMAIL: RAQUELGIANA33@GMAIL.COM)

DEFENDANT STEFFEN WEISS' Motion To Seal Records Pursuant To Protective Order.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant's motion to seal records is granted. No opposition filed, and the Court finds that the requirements of Cal. R. Ct. 2.550 are met.

Defendant shall prepare an order that identifies the documents to be filed under seal [Shah Decl., Exs. THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - Not argued. The matter is Discussed (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PREMISE DATA CORPORATION's Amended Notice And Motion To Compel:

The motion is GRANTED. The deposition questions at issue are relevant and a plausible line of inquiry. The defendant's answers - whether deliberately or not - were non-responsive to the key component of the question(s) namely, the timing of the onset of the elements of his emotional distress. He continually answered the question with the substance - or, the "what" - of statements made to his therapist, but never the "when" insofar as the timing of the onset of those symptoms. Therefore, counsel's objections and instructions were improper and the plaintiff is entitled to full and complete response.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz, Clerk: S. Le, Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR# 10619, of Barkley Court Reporters, (Tel: 925-381-5000; Email: raquelgiana33@gmail.com)
